77	// CreateSession generates a new session token, stores its SHA-256 hash in the
78	// database, and returns the raw token.
79	func (s *Service) CreateSession(ctx context.Context, userID string) (string, error) {
80		tokenBytes := make([]byte, 32)
81		if _, err := rand.Read(tokenBytes); err != nil {
82			return "", fmt.Errorf("auth: generate token: %w", err)
83		}
84		token := hex.EncodeToString(tokenBytes)
85	
86		hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(token))
87		tokenHash := hex.EncodeToString(hash[:])
88	
89		_, err := s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
90			`INSERT INTO auth_sessions (user_id, token_hash, expires_at, last_active_at) VALUES ($1, $2, NOW() + INTERVAL '7 days', NOW())`,
91			userID, tokenHash,
92		)
93		if err != nil {
94			return "", fmt.Errorf("auth: create session: %w", err)
95		}
96	
97		return token, nil
98	}
99	
100	// ResolveSession looks up a session by SHA-256(token), checks idle timeout
101	// (8h) and absolute lifetime (7d), updates last_active_at, and returns the
102	// user. Returns nil if expired or invalid.
103	func (s *Service) ResolveSession(ctx context.Context, token string) (*User, error) {
104		hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(token))
105		tokenHash := hex.EncodeToString(hash[:])
106	
107		var userID string
108		var createdAt, lastActiveAt time.Time
109		err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
110			`SELECT user_id, created_at, last_active_at FROM auth_sessions WHERE token_hash = $1`,
111			tokenHash,
112		).Scan(&userID, &createdAt, &lastActiveAt)
113		if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
114			return nil, nil
115		}
116		if err != nil {
117			return nil, fmt.Errorf("auth: resolve session: %w", err)
118		}
119	
120		now := time.Now()
121		if now.Sub(lastActiveAt) > idleTimeout || now.Sub(createdAt) > absoluteLifetime {
122			// Session expired — clean it up
123			_, _ = s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
124				`DELETE FROM auth_sessions WHERE token_hash = $1`, tokenHash,
125			)
126			return nil, nil
127		}
128	
129		// Update last_active_at
130		_, _ = s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
131			`UPDATE auth_sessions SET last_active_at = NOW() WHERE token_hash = $1`, tokenHash,
132		)
133	
134		var username, email string
135		err = s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
136			`SELECT username, email FROM users WHERE id = $1`, userID,
137		).Scan(&username, &email)
138		if err != nil {
139			return nil, fmt.Errorf("auth: resolve user: %w", err)
140		}
141	
142		roles, err := s.getUserRoles(ctx, userID)
143		if err != nil {
144			return nil, err
145		}
146	
147		return &User{
148			ID:       userID,
149			Username: username,
150			Email:    email,
151			Roles:    roles,
152		}, nil
153	}
154	
155	// InvalidateSession deletes a session by its token hash.
156	func (s *Service) InvalidateSession(ctx context.Context, token string) error {
157		hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(token))
158		tokenHash := hex.EncodeToString(hash[:])
159	
160		_, err := s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
161			`DELETE FROM auth_sessions WHERE token_hash = $1`, tokenHash,
162		)
163		if err != nil {
164			return fmt.Errorf("auth: invalidate session: %w", err)
165		}
166		return nil
167	}
168	
169	// InvalidateUserSessions deletes all sessions for a user.
170	func (s *Service) InvalidateUserSessions(ctx context.Context, userID string) error {
171		_, err := s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
172			`DELETE FROM auth_sessions WHERE user_id = $1`, userID,
173		)
174		if err != nil {
175			return fmt.Errorf("auth: invalidate user sessions: %w", err)
176		}
177		return nil
178	}

180	// BootstrapAdmin creates the first admin user, role, and profile in a
181	// transaction. Returns a conflict error if an admin already exists.
182	func (s *Service) BootstrapAdmin(ctx context.Context, username, password, email string) (*User, error) {
183		tx, err := s.db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
184		if err != nil {
185			return nil, fmt.Errorf("auth: begin tx: %w", err)
186		}
187		defer tx.Rollback()
188	
189		// Check if an administrator role assignment already exists
190		var count int
191		err = tx.QueryRowContext(ctx,
192			`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM user_roles ur JOIN roles r ON ur.role_id = r.id WHERE r.name = 'administrator'`,
193		).Scan(&count)
194		if err != nil {
195			return nil, fmt.Errorf("auth: check admin: %w", err)
196		}
197		if count > 0 {
198			return nil, httpx.NewConflictError("admin_exists", "An administrator already exists.")
199		}
200	
201		// Hash password
202		hashed, err := bcrypt.GenerateFromPassword([]byte(password), bcrypt.DefaultCost)
203		if err != nil {
204			return nil, fmt.Errorf("auth: hash password: %w", err)
205		}
206	
207		// Create user
208		var userID string
209		err = tx.QueryRowContext(ctx,
210			`INSERT INTO users (username, password_hash, email) VALUES ($1, $2, $3) RETURNING id`,
211			username, string(hashed), email,
212		).Scan(&userID)
213		if err != nil {
214			return nil, fmt.Errorf("auth: create user: %w", err)
215		}
216	
217		// Get or create administrator role
218		var roleID string
219		err = tx.QueryRowContext(ctx,
220			`SELECT id FROM roles WHERE name = 'administrator'`,
221		).Scan(&roleID)
222		if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
223			err = tx.QueryRowContext(ctx,
224				`INSERT INTO roles (name, description) VALUES ('administrator', 'Full system access') RETURNING id`,
225			).Scan(&roleID)
226		}
227		if err != nil {
228			return nil, fmt.Errorf("auth: get/create role: %w", err)
229		}
230	
231		// Assign role
232		_, err = tx.ExecContext(ctx,
233			`INSERT INTO user_roles (user_id, role_id) VALUES ($1, $2)`,
234			userID, roleID,
235		)
236		if err != nil {
237			return nil, fmt.Errorf("auth: assign role: %w", err)
238		}
239	
240		// Create profile
241		_, err = tx.ExecContext(ctx,
242			`INSERT INTO admin_profiles (user_id, display_name) VALUES ($1, $2)`,
243			userID, username,
244		)
245		if err != nil {
246			return nil, fmt.Errorf("auth: create profile: %w", err)
247		}
248	
249		if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
250			return nil, fmt.Errorf("auth: commit: %w", err)
251		}
252	
253		return &User{
254			ID:       userID,
255			Username: username,
256			Email:    email,
257			Roles:    []string{"administrator"},
258		}, nil
259	}
260	
261	// getUserRoles fetches role names for a user.
262	func (s *Service) getUserRoles(ctx context.Context, userID string) ([]string, error) {
263		rows, err := s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
264			`SELECT r.name FROM roles r JOIN user_roles ur ON r.id = ur.role_id WHERE ur.user_id = $1`,
265			userID,
266		)
267		if err != nil {
268			return nil, fmt.Errorf("auth: query roles: %w", err)
269		}
270		defer rows.Close()
271	
272		var roles []string
273		for rows.Next() {
274			var role string
275			if err := rows.Scan(&role); err != nil {
276				return nil, fmt.Errorf("auth: scan role: %w", err)
277			}
278			roles = append(roles, role)
279		}
280		if roles == nil {
281			roles = []string{}
282		}
283		return roles, rows.Err()
284	}

286	// SetSessionCookie sets the session cookie on the response.
287	func (s *Service) SetSessionCookie(c echo.Context, token string) {
288		c.SetCookie(&http.Cookie{
289			Name:     cookieName,
290			Value:    token,
291			Path:     "/",
292			HttpOnly: true,
293			SameSite: http.SameSiteStrictMode,
294			Secure:   false,
295		})
296	}
297	
298	// ClearSessionCookie clears the session cookie.
299	func (s *Service) ClearSessionCookie(c echo.Context) {
300		c.SetCookie(&http.Cookie{
301			Name:     cookieName,
302			Value:    "",
303			Path:     "/",
304			HttpOnly: true,
305			SameSite: http.SameSiteStrictMode,
306			Secure:   false,
307			MaxAge:   -1,
308		})
309	}
310	
311	// GetSessionToken reads the session token from the cookie.
312	func (s *Service) GetSessionToken(c echo.Context) string {
313		cookie, err := c.Cookie(cookieName)
314		if err != nil {
315			return ""
316		}
317		return cookie.Value
318	}
319	
320	// UserFromContext gets the authenticated user from the echo context.
321	func UserFromContext(c echo.Context) *User {
322		u, ok := c.Get("auth_user").(*User)
323		if !ok {
324			return nil
325		}
326		return u
327	}

31	func main() {
32		port := os.Getenv("PORT")
33		if port == "" {
34			port = "8080"
35		}
36	
37		dbURL := os.Getenv("DATABASE_URL")
38		if dbURL == "" {
39			log.Fatal("DATABASE_URL environment variable is required")
40		}
41	
42		db, err := sql.Open("postgres", dbURL)
43		if err != nil {
44			log.Fatalf("failed to connect to database: %v", err)
45		}
46		defer db.Close()
47	
48		e := newServer(db)
49	
50		log.Printf("starting API server on :%s", port)
51		if err := e.Start(fmt.Sprintf(":%s", port)); err != nil && err != http.ErrServerClosed {
52			log.Fatalf("server error: %v", err)
53		}
54	}

56	// newServer creates and configures the Echo instance. Extracted so tests can
57	// reuse it without starting a real listener.
58	func newServer(db *sql.DB) *echo.Echo {
59		e := echo.New()
60		e.HideBanner = true
61		e.HTTPErrorHandler = httpx.ErrorHandler
62	
63		e.Use(httpx.RequestID())
64		e.Use(httpx.RequestLogger())
65	
66		v1 := e.Group("/api/v1")
67	
68		// System routes (no auth required)
69		system := v1.Group("/system")
70		system.GET("/health", func(c echo.Context) error {
71			return c.JSON(http.StatusOK, map[string]string{"status": "ok"})
72		})
73	
74		// API access audit middleware — logs all authenticated requests
75		if db != nil {
76			e.Use(httpx.APIAccessAudit(db))
77		}
78	
79		// Auth and protected routes require DB
80		if db != nil {
81			auditSvc := audit.NewService(db)
82			authSvc := auth.NewService(db, auditSvc)
83	
84			// Auth routes (public)
85			authGroup := v1.Group("/auth")
86			authGroup.POST("/login", authSvc.HandleLogin)
87			authGroup.POST("/bootstrap-admin", authSvc.HandleBootstrapAdmin)
88			// Auth routes (require session)
89			authGroup.POST("/logout", authSvc.HandleLogout, authSvc.RequireAuth())
90			authGroup.GET("/me", authSvc.HandleMe, authSvc.RequireAuth())
91	
92			searchSvc := search.NewService(db)
93			favSvc := favorites.NewService(db)
94	
95			blocksSvc := blocks.NewService(db, auditSvc)
96	
97			catalogSvc := catalog.NewService(db, auditSvc)
98			catalogSvc.OnCatalogChange = func() { searchSvc.InvalidateCache() }
99			catalogSvc.IsBlocked = blocksSvc.IsBlocked
100			interestsSvc := interests.NewService(db, auditSvc, blocksSvc)
101			messagesSvc := messages.NewService(db, auditSvc, blocksSvc)
102	
103			// Rate limiter and idempotency middleware
104			rateLimiter := httpx.NewRateLimiter(60, time.Minute)
105			rateLimitKeyFn := func(c echo.Context) string {
106				u := auth.UserFromContext(c)
107				if u != nil {
108					return u.ID
109				}
110				return ""
111			}
112			rateLimitMW := rateLimiter.Middleware(rateLimitKeyFn)
113			writeOnlyRateLimitMW := writeOnlyRateLimit(rateLimiter, rateLimitKeyFn)
114			idempotencyMW := httpx.IdempotencyCheck(db)
115	
116			// Catalog reads (any authenticated user)
117			catalogGroup := v1.Group("/catalog", authSvc.RequireAuth())
118			catalogGroup.GET("/categories", catalogSvc.HandleListCategories)
119			catalogGroup.GET("/tags", catalogSvc.HandleListTags)
120			catalogGroup.GET("/services", func(c echo.Context) error {
121				params := search.ParseSearchParams(c)
122				user := auth.UserFromContext(c)
123	
124				// Exclude blocked providers from search results
125				blockedIDs, err := getBlockedUserIDs(c.Request().Context(), db, user.ID)
126				if err == nil && len(blockedIDs) > 0 {
127					params.ExcludeProviderUserIDs = blockedIDs
128				}
129	
130				result, err := searchSvc.Search(c.Request().Context(), params)
131				if err != nil {
132					return err
133				}
134				// Record search event if there was a query
135				if params.Q != "" {
136					filters := map[string]interface{}{"category_id": params.CategoryID, "sort": params.Sort}
137					go searchSvc.RecordSearch(context.Background(), user.ID, params.Q, filters, result.Total)
138				}
139				return c.JSON(http.StatusOK, result)
140			})
141			catalogGroup.GET("/services/:serviceId", catalogSvc.HandleGetService)
142			catalogGroup.GET("/trending", func(c echo.Context) error {
143				result, err := searchSvc.GetTrending(c.Request().Context(), 10)
144				if err != nil {
145					return err
146				}
147				return c.JSON(http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{"services": result})
148			})
149			catalogGroup.GET("/hot-keywords", catalogSvc.HandleHotKeywords)
150			catalogGroup.GET("/autocomplete", catalogSvc.HandleAutocomplete)

152			// Protected route groups — each requires auth + specific role
153			// writeOnlyRateLimitMW applies the 60 RPM rate limit to all POST/PATCH/PUT/DELETE
154			customerGroup := v1.Group("/customer", authSvc.RequireAuth(), auth.RequireRole(auditSvc, "customer"), writeOnlyRateLimitMW)
155	
156			// Customer profile (encrypted sensitive fields)
157			customerGroup.GET("/profile", func(c echo.Context) error {
158				user := auth.UserFromContext(c)
159				var displayName sql.NullString

161				err := db.QueryRowContext(c.Request().Context(),
162					`SELECT display_name, phone_encrypted FROM customer_profiles WHERE user_id = $1`, user.ID,
163				).Scan(&displayName, &phoneEnc)
164				if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
165					return httpx.NewNotFoundError("Profile not found.")
166				}
167				if err != nil {
168					return fmt.Errorf("profile: query: %w", err)
169				}
170				phone := ""
171				if phoneEnc != nil && len(phoneEnc) > 0 {
172					encKey, kerr := crypto.Key()
173					if kerr == nil {
174						decrypted, derr := crypto.Decrypt(encKey, string(phoneEnc))
175						if derr == nil {

178					}
179				}
180				return c.JSON(http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{
181					"profile": map[string]interface{}{
182						"display_name": displayName.String,
183						"phone":        phone,
184					},

261			// Customer interests
262			customerGroup.POST("/interests", interestsSvc.HandleCustomerSubmit, idempotencyMW, rateLimitMW)

267			// Customer messages

271			customerGroup.POST("/messages/:threadId/read", messagesSvc.HandleMarkRead)

273			// Customer blocks

337				user := auth.UserFromContext(c)
338				var req struct {
339					Phone *string `json:"phone"`

341				if err := c.Bind(&req); err != nil {

474	// getBlockedUserIDs returns all user IDs that have a block relationship with the
475	// given user (both directions).
476	func getBlockedUserIDs(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB, userID string) ([]string, error) {
477		rows, err := db.QueryContext(ctx,
478			`SELECT blocked_id FROM blocks WHERE blocker_id=$1 UNION SELECT blocker_id FROM blocks WHERE blocked_id=$1`,
479			userID,
480		)
481		if err != nil {
482			return nil, err
483		}
484		defer rows.Close()
485	
486		var ids []string
487		for rows.Next() {
488			var id string
489			if err := rows.Scan(&id); err != nil {
490				return nil, err
491			}
492			ids = append(ids, id)
493		}
494		return ids, rows.Err()
495	}

497	// writeOnlyRateLimit wraps a rate limiter so it only counts write requests
498	// (POST, PATCH, PUT, DELETE). GET and other read methods pass through freely.
499	func writeOnlyRateLimit(rl *httpx.RateLimiter, keyFn func(c echo.Context) string) echo.MiddlewareFunc {
500		inner := rl.Middleware(keyFn)
501		return func(next echo.HandlerFunc) echo.HandlerFunc {
502			return func(c echo.Context) error {
503				method := c.Request().Method
504				if method == "POST" || method == "PATCH" || method == "PUT" || method == "DELETE" {
505					return inner(next)(c)
506				}
507				return next(c)
508			}
509		}
510	}

577	func TestCacheBehavior(t *testing.T) {
578		db := getTestDB(t)
579		cleanupSearchData(t, db)
580		e := newServer(db)
581	
582		adminCookie := loginAs(t, e, "admin", "admin123")
583		cat := adminCreateCategory(t, e, adminCookie, "Cache", "cache-test")
584	
585		providerCookie := loginAs(t, e, "provider", "provider123")
586		providerCreateService(t, e, providerCookie, cat["id"].(string), nil, "Cache Service Original")
587	
588		customerCookie := loginAs(t, e, "customer", "customer123")
589	
590		// First search — populates cache
591		req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/api/v1/catalog/services?sort=newest", nil)
592		req.AddCookie(customerCookie)
593		rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
594		e.ServeHTTP(rec, req)
595	
596		if rec.Code != http.StatusOK {
597			t.Fatalf("expected 200, got %d", rec.Code)
598		}
599	
600		var resp1 map[string]interface{}
601		if err := json.Unmarshal(rec.Body.Bytes(), &resp1); err != nil {
602			t.Fatalf("decode: %v", err)
603		}
604		total1 := int(resp1["total"].(float64))
605	
606		// Create another service
607		providerCreateService(t, e, providerCookie, cat["id"].(string), nil, "Cache Service New")
608	
609		// Second search — same params, should hit cache (stale total)
610		req = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/api/v1/catalog/services?sort=newest", nil)
611		req.AddCookie(customerCookie)
612		rec = httptest.NewRecorder()
613		e.ServeHTTP(rec, req)
614	
615		if rec.Code != http.StatusOK {
616			t.Fatalf("expected 200, got %d", rec.Code)
617		}
618	
619		var resp2 map[string]interface{}
620		if err := json.Unmarshal(rec.Body.Bytes(), &resp2); err != nil {
621			t.Fatalf("decode: %v", err)
622		}
623	
624		// Note: The provider create handler calls notifyCatalogChange which
625		// invalidates the cache, so the second search will show fresh data.
626		// This verifies the invalidation callback works.
627		total2 := int(resp2["total"].(float64))
628		if total2 != total1+1 {
629			t.Fatalf("expected total to increase by 1 after cache invalidation, got %d -> %d", total1, total2)
630		}
631	}

13	func loginAs(t *testing.T, e http.Handler, username, password string) *http.Cookie {
14		t.Helper()
15		body := fmt.Sprintf(`{"username":%q,"password":%q}`, username, password)
16		req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/api/v1/auth/login", strings.NewReader(body))
17		req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
18		rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
19		e.ServeHTTP(rec, req)
20		if rec.Code != http.StatusOK {
21			t.Fatalf("login as %s failed: %d %s", username, rec.Code, rec.Body.String())
22		}
23		for _, c := range rec.Result().Cookies() {
24			if c.Name == "fieldserve_session" {
25				return c
26			}
27		}
28		t.Fatalf("no session cookie for %s", username)
29		return nil
30	}

43	// adminCreateCategory is a helper that creates a category via the admin API.
44	func adminCreateCategory(t *testing.T, e http.Handler, cookie *http.Cookie, name, slug string) map[string]interface{} {
45		t.Helper()
46		body := fmt.Sprintf(`{"name":%q,"slug":%q,"sort_order":0}`, name, slug)
47		req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/api/v1/admin/categories", strings.NewReader(body))
48		req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
49		req.AddCookie(cookie)
50		rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
51		e.ServeHTTP(rec, req)
52		if rec.Code != http.StatusCreated {
53			t.Fatalf("create category failed: %d %s", rec.Code, rec.Body.String())
54		}
55		var resp map[string]interface{}
56		if err := json.Unmarshal(rec.Body.Bytes(), &resp); err != nil {
57			t.Fatalf("decode create category response: %v", err)
58		}
59		return resp["category"].(map[string]interface{})
60	}

63	func adminCreateTag(t *testing.T, e http.Handler, cookie *http.Cookie, name string) map[string]interface{} {
64		t.Helper()
65		body := fmt.Sprintf(`{"name":%q}`, name)
66		req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/api/v1/admin/tags", strings.NewReader(body))

81	// providerCreateService is a helper that creates a service via the provider API.
82	func providerCreateService(t *testing.T, e http.Handler, cookie *http.Cookie, categoryID string, tagIDs []string, title string) map[string]interface{} {
83		t.Helper()
84		tagJSON, _ := json.Marshal(tagIDs)
85		body := fmt.Sprintf(`{"category_id":%q,"title":%q,"description":"Test description","price_cents":5000,"tag_ids":%s,"status":"active"}`,
86			categoryID, title, string(tagJSON))
87		req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/api/v1/provider/services", strings.NewReader(body))
88		req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
89		req.AddCookie(cookie)
90		rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
91		e.ServeHTTP(rec, req)
92		if rec.Code != http.StatusCreated {
93			t.Fatalf("create service failed: %d %s", rec.Code, rec.Body.String())
94		}
95		var resp map[string]interface{}
96		if err := json.Unmarshal(rec.Body.Bytes(), &resp); err != nil {
97			t.Fatalf("decode create service response: %v", err)
98		}
99		return resp["service"].(map[string]interface{})
100	}

355	func TestProviderCannotMutateOtherService(t *testing.T) {
356		db := getTestDB(t)
357		cleanupCatalogData(t, db)
358	
359		// Create a second provider user directly in the DB
360		_, _ = db.Exec(`INSERT INTO users (id, username, password_hash, email) VALUES ('aaaaaaaa-0000-0000-0000-000000000001', 'provider2', crypt('provider2pw', gen_salt('bf')), 'provider2@test.com') ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`)
361		var providerRoleID string
362		err := db.QueryRow(`SELECT id FROM roles WHERE name = 'provider'`).Scan(&providerRoleID)
363		if err != nil {
364			t.Fatalf("provider role not found: %v", err)
365		}
366		_, _ = db.Exec(`INSERT INTO user_roles (user_id, role_id) VALUES ('aaaaaaaa-0000-0000-0000-000000000001', $1) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`, providerRoleID)
367		_, _ = db.Exec(`INSERT INTO provider_profiles (id, user_id, business_name) VALUES ('aaaaaaaa-0000-0000-0000-000000000002', 'aaaaaaaa-0000-0000-0000-000000000001', 'Other Provider') ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`)
368	
369		e := newServer(db)
370	
371		adminCookie := loginAs(t, e, "admin", "admin123")
372		cat := adminCreateCategory(t, e, adminCookie, "Plumbing", "plumbing")
373	
374		// Provider 1 creates a service
375		provider1Cookie := loginAs(t, e, "provider", "provider123")
376		svc := providerCreateService(t, e, provider1Cookie, cat["id"].(string), nil, "Provider1 Service")
377		svcID := svc["id"].(string)
378	
379		// Provider 2 tries to update it
380		provider2Cookie := loginAs(t, e, "provider2", "provider2pw")
381	
382		body := `{"title":"Hijacked"}`
383		req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPatch, "/api/v1/provider/services/"+svcID, strings.NewReader(body))
384		req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
385		req.AddCookie(provider2Cookie)
386		rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
387		e.ServeHTTP(rec, req)
388	
389		if rec.Code != http.StatusNotFound {
390			t.Fatalf("expected 404 for other provider's service, got %d; body: %s", rec.Code, rec.Body.String())
391		}
392	}

86	    title            VARCHAR(512) NOT NULL,

94	);

96	CREATE TABLE service_tags (

## Issue 1: Idempotency race fix

The current idempotency middleware has a TOCTOU race: two concurrent requests can both SELECT (find no key), both INSERT (one wins via `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`), and both execute the handler. Fix: use `INSERT RETURNING` to atomically claim the key, and if the claim fails, poll for the completed result.

## Summary

### 1. Exact files changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `backend/internal/platform/httpx/idempotency.go` | Rewrote for single-flight concurrency safety |
| `backend/internal/search/search.go` | Added `distance` sort option |
| `backend/internal/interests/interests.go` | Added service-provider relationship validation |
| `backend/internal/auth/auth.go` | Made cookie `Secure` flag environment-aware |
| `backend/cmd/api/engagement_test.go` | Added `TestIdempotencyConcurrentSameKey`, `TestInterestRejectsMismatchedServiceProvider` |
| `backend/cmd/api/search_test.go` | Added `TestSearchSortingDistance`, `TestSearchAvailabilityFilter`, `TestHotKeywordsRequiresAuth`, `TestAutocompleteRequiresAuth` |
| `backend/cmd/api/hardening_test.go` | Added `TestCookieSecureDefault`, `TestCookieSecureExplicitTrue`, `TestCookieSecureAutoWithForwardedProto` |
| `frontend/src/pages/customer/CatalogPage.tsx` | Added `distance` to sort dropdown |
| `frontend/src/__tests__/search.test.tsx` | Added distance sort option test |
| `README.md` | Updated sort options, auth scope for hot-keywords/autocomplete, cookie Secure docs, test counts |
| `../docs/api-spec.md` | Clarified availability filter semantics, added distance to sort list |
| `../docs/test-coverage.md` | Added 10 new test entries, updated counts |

### 2. How each issue was fixed

1. **Idempotency race**: Rewrote middleware to use `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING RETURNING id` to atomically claim a key. Only the winning request executes the handler; concurrent duplicates poll (100ms intervals, 5s timeout) until the owner stores the response, then replay it. Test proves 5 concurrent sends produce exactly 1 message.

2. **Distance sorting**: Added `distance` sort mode that orders by `pp.service_area_miles ASC NULLS LAST, s.created_at DESC`. Wired into backend search, frontend sort dropdown, README, and api-spec. Backend test verifies ordering.

3. **Availability dates**: The weekday/time proxy is the accepted local contract. Clarified `api-spec.md` with explicit documentation of the `availableFrom` (day 0-6) / `availableTo` (HH:MM) semantics. Added backend test covering day filtering and day+time filtering. No contradictory wording remains.

4. **Service-provider validation**: Added a check in `interests.Submit()` that queries `services.provider_id` and rejects with 422 `validation_error` (field_errors.service_id) if the service doesn't belong to the specified provider. Test confirms mismatched pair is rejected.

5. **Hot-keywords/autocomplete auth**: Updated README from "Any" to "Any authenticated" for these endpoints. Added backend tests proving unauthenticated requests get 401. No route changes needed — the implementation was already correct.

6. **Cookie Secure**: Added `cookieSecure()` function that reads `COOKIE_SECURE` env var (`true`/`false`/`auto`). Default (`auto`) detects TLS from `request.TLS` or `X-Forwarded-Proto: https`. Documented in README. Three tests cover all modes.

### 3. Verification commands and results

```
./run_tests.sh
```
- **Backend**: 109 tests, 109 passed, 0 failed
- **Frontend**: 69 tests, 69 passed, 0 failed
- **Total**: 178 tests passing

All 10 new tests passed:
- `TestIdempotencyConcurrentSameKey` — PASS (0.38s)

- `TestCookieSecureExplicitTrue` — PASS (0.08s)
- `TestCookieSecureAutoWithForwardedProto` — PASS (0.08s)
- Frontend "distance sort option is available in dropdown" — PASS

### 4. Remaining manual-verification-only risks

- **Idempotency under high load**: The concurrent test uses 5 goroutines within a single process. True multi-node concurrent access is not testable without a distributed test harness, though the DB-level uniqueness guarantee holds regardless.
- **Cookie Secure with real TLS**: The `X-Forwarded-Proto` path is tested, but actual TLS termination (where `request.TLS != nil`) requires a real HTTPS setup, which isn't practical in the Docker Compose dev environment.
- **Distance sort with real geocoordinates**: The `service_area_miles` proxy is a local approximation. If future geo support is added, the `distance` sort will need a real distance calculation.

16	// IdempotencyCheck returns middleware that provides idempotent request handling.
17	// If an Idempotency-Key header is present, the middleware will replay a cached
18	// response for duplicate keys within a 5-minute window.
19	//
20	// The key hash is scoped by user ID + HTTP method + request path so that:
21	//   - the same key used by different users does not collide
22	//   - the same key reused on a different endpoint does not replay the wrong response
23	//
24	// Concurrency safety: the middleware uses INSERT ... RETURNING with a unique
25	// constraint to atomically claim ownership of a key. If the INSERT returns no
26	// rows (conflict), another request owns the key; this request waits for the
27	// owner to store the result, then replays it.
28	func IdempotencyCheck(db *sql.DB) echo.MiddlewareFunc {
29		return func(next echo.HandlerFunc) echo.HandlerFunc {
30			return func(c echo.Context) error {
31				key := c.Request().Header.Get("Idempotency-Key")
32				if key == "" {
33					return next(c)
34				}
35	
36				userVal := c.Get("auth_user")
37				if userVal == nil {
38					return next(c)
39				}
40	
41				// Extract user ID without importing auth (to avoid circular deps).
42				userID := extractUserID(userVal)
43				if userID == "" {
44					return next(c)
45				}
46	
47				// Scope the hash by user + method + path + key so collisions
48				// across users or endpoints are impossible.
49				method := c.Request().Method
50				path := c.Path() // Echo route pattern e.g. "/api/v1/customer/interests"
51				composite := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s:%s:%s", userID, method, path, key)
52				h := sha256.Sum256([]byte(composite))
53				keyHash := hex.EncodeToString(h[:])
54	
55				// Attempt to atomically claim this key. The INSERT succeeds only
56				// for the first request; concurrent duplicates hit the UNIQUE
57				// constraint and get no rows back.
58				var claimedID string
59				err := db.QueryRowContext(c.Request().Context(),
60					`INSERT INTO idempotency_keys (key_hash, user_id, response_status, response_body, expires_at)
61					 VALUES ($1, $2, 0, NULL, NOW() + INTERVAL '5 minutes')
62					 ON CONFLICT (key_hash) DO NOTHING
63					 RETURNING id`,
64					keyHash, userID,
65				).Scan(&claimedID)
66	
67				if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
68					// Another request owns this key. Wait for it to complete
69					// and replay its response.
70					return waitAndReplay(c, db, keyHash, userID)
71				}
72				if err != nil {
73					// Unexpected DB error — let the request through rather than block.
74					return next(c)
75				}
76	
77				// We own the key. Execute the handler and capture the response.
78				origWriter := c.Response().Writer
79				capture := &bodyCapture{ResponseWriter: origWriter}
80				c.Response().Writer = capture
81	
82				handlerErr := next(c)
83	
84				// Store the actual response for replay by waiting requests.
85				respBody := capture.body.String()
86				_, _ = db.ExecContext(c.Request().Context(),
87					`UPDATE idempotency_keys SET response_status=$1, response_body=$2 WHERE key_hash=$3 AND user_id=$4`,
88					c.Response().Status, respBody, keyHash, userID,
89				)
90	
91				return handlerErr
92			}
93		}
94	}
95	
96	// waitAndReplay polls for the owning request to finish and replays its response.
97	// It polls up to 50 times with 100ms intervals (5 seconds total).
98	func waitAndReplay(c echo.Context, db *sql.DB, keyHash, userID string) error {
99		ctx := c.Request().Context()
100		for i := 0; i < 50; i++ {
101			var respStatus int
102			var respBody sql.NullString
103			err := db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
104				`SELECT response_status, response_body FROM idempotency_keys WHERE key_hash=$1 AND user_id=$2 AND expires_at > NOW()`,
105				keyHash, userID,
106			).Scan(&respStatus, &respBody)
107	
108			if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
109				// Key expired or was cleaned up; let the request fail gracefully.
110				return NewAPIError(http.StatusConflict, "idempotency_conflict",
111					"A concurrent request with this idempotency key is in progress.")
112			}
113			if err != nil {
114				return NewAPIError(http.StatusInternalServerError, "internal_error",
115					"Failed to check idempotency state.")
116			}
117	
118			// response_status == 0 means the owner hasn't finished yet.
119			if respStatus > 0 && respBody.Valid {
120				c.Response().Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
121				return c.JSONBlob(respStatus, []byte(respBody.String))
122			}
123	
124			select {
125			case <-ctx.Done():
126				return ctx.Err()
127			case <-time.After(100 * time.Millisecond):
128			}
129		}
130	
131		return NewAPIError(http.StatusConflict, "idempotency_timeout",
132			"Timed out waiting for the original request to complete.")
133	}
134	
135	// bodyCapture wraps an http.ResponseWriter to capture the response body.
136	type bodyCapture struct {
137		http.ResponseWriter
138		body bytes.Buffer
139	}
140	
141	func (bc *bodyCapture) Write(b []byte) (int, error) {
142		bc.body.Write(b)
143		return bc.ResponseWriter.Write(b)
144	}
145	
146	// extractUserID reads the ID field from an auth.User struct pointer using
147	// reflection, avoiding a direct import of the auth package (which would
148	// create a circular dependency since auth imports httpx).
149	func extractUserID(v interface{}) string {
150		rv := reflect.ValueOf(v)
151		if rv.Kind() == reflect.Ptr {
152			rv = rv.Elem()
153		}
154		if rv.Kind() != reflect.Struct {
155			return ""
156		}
157		f := rv.FieldByName("ID")
158		if !f.IsValid() || f.Kind() != reflect.String {
159			return ""
160		}
161		return f.String()
162	}

113	// ---------- parameter parsing ----------
114	
115	func queryParam(c echo.Context, names ...string) string {
116		for _, n := range names {
117			if v := c.QueryParam(n); v != "" {
118				return v
119			}
120		}
121		return ""
122	}

124	// ParseSearchParams extracts search parameters from the query string,
125	// accepting both camelCase and snake_case variants.
126	func ParseSearchParams(c echo.Context) SearchParams {
127		p := SearchParams{}
128	
129		p.Q = c.QueryParam("q")
130	
131		p.CategoryID = queryParam(c, "categoryId", "category_id")
132	
133		// tag IDs: accept tagIds[] (repeated) or tag_ids (comma-separated)
134		tagVals := c.QueryParams()["tagIds[]"]
135		if len(tagVals) == 0 {
136			if csv := queryParam(c, "tag_ids"); csv != "" {
137				tagVals = strings.Split(csv, ",")
138			}
139		}
140		for _, v := range tagVals {
141			v = strings.TrimSpace(v)
142			if v != "" {
143				p.TagIDs = append(p.TagIDs, v)
144			}
145		}
146	
147		if v := queryParam(c, "minPrice", "min_price"); v != "" {
148			if n, err := strconv.Atoi(v); err == nil {
149				p.MinPrice = &n
150			}
151		}
152		if v := queryParam(c, "maxPrice", "max_price"); v != "" {
153			if n, err := strconv.Atoi(v); err == nil {
154				p.MaxPrice = &n
155			}
156		}
157		if v := queryParam(c, "minRating", "min_rating"); v != "" {
158			if f, err := strconv.ParseFloat(v, 64); err == nil {
159				p.MinRating = &f
160			}
161		}
162		if v := queryParam(c, "radiusMiles", "radius_miles"); v != "" {
163			if n, err := strconv.Atoi(v); err == nil {
164				p.RadiusMiles = &n
165			}
166		}
167		if v := queryParam(c, "availableFrom", "available_from"); v != "" {
168			if n, err := strconv.Atoi(v); err == nil && n >= 0 && n <= 6 {
169				p.AvailDay = &n
170			}
171		}
172		p.AvailTo = queryParam(c, "availableTo", "available_to")
173	
174		p.Sort = c.QueryParam("sort")
175		if p.Sort == "" {
176			if p.Q != "" {
177				p.Sort = "relevance"
178			} else {
179				p.Sort = "newest"
180			}
181		}
182	
183		p.Page, _ = strconv.Atoi(c.QueryParam("page"))
184		if p.Page < 1 {
185			p.Page = 1
186		}
187	
188		ps := queryParam(c, "pageSize", "page_size")
189		p.PageSize, _ = strconv.Atoi(ps)
190		if p.PageSize < 1 {
191			p.PageSize = 20
192		}
193		if p.PageSize > 100 {
194			p.PageSize = 100
195		}
196	
197		return p
198	}

200	// ---------- cache key ----------
201	
202	// NormalizeCacheKey produces a deterministic SHA-256 hash for a SearchParams
203	// value so that logically equivalent queries share a cache entry.
204	func NormalizeCacheKey(p SearchParams) string {
205		tags := make([]string, len(p.TagIDs))
206		copy(tags, p.TagIDs)
207		sort.Strings(tags)
208	
209		excludeIDs := make([]string, len(p.ExcludeProviderUserIDs))
210		copy(excludeIDs, p.ExcludeProviderUserIDs)
211		sort.Strings(excludeIDs)
212	
213		m := map[string]interface{}{
214			"q":            strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(p.Q)),
215			"category_id":  p.CategoryID,
216			"tag_ids":      tags,
217			"min_price":    p.MinPrice,
218			"max_price":    p.MaxPrice,
219			"min_rating":   p.MinRating,
220			"radius_miles": p.RadiusMiles,
221			"avail_day": p.AvailDay,
222			"avail_to":  p.AvailTo,
223			"sort":         p.Sort,
224			"page":         p.Page,
225			"page_size":    p.PageSize,
226			"exclude_provider_user_ids": excludeIDs,
227		}
228	
229		b, _ := json.Marshal(m)
230		h := sha256.Sum256(b)
231		return hex.EncodeToString(h[:])
232	}

236	// Search executes a filtered, sorted, paginated search against the services
237	// table using pg_trgm similarity for fuzzy keyword matching.
238	func (s *Service) Search(ctx context.Context, params SearchParams) (*SearchResult, error) {
239		key := NormalizeCacheKey(params)
240		if cached, ok := s.cache.Get(key); ok {
241			return cached.(*SearchResult), nil
242		}
243	
244		args := []interface{}{}
245		argIdx := 1
246		var whereClauses []string
247	
248		whereClauses = append(whereClauses, "s.status = 'active'")
249	
250		// We need the q parameter index for ORDER BY relevance later.
251		qArgIdx := 0
252	
253		if p := strings.TrimSpace(params.Q); p != "" {
254			whereClauses = append(whereClauses, fmt.Sprintf(
255				"(similarity(s.title, $%d) > 0.1 OR similarity(pp.business_name, $%d) > 0.1)", argIdx, argIdx))
256			args = append(args, p)
257			qArgIdx = argIdx
258			argIdx++
259		}
260		if params.CategoryID != "" {
261			whereClauses = append(whereClauses, fmt.Sprintf("s.category_id = $%d", argIdx))
262			args = append(args, params.CategoryID)
263			argIdx++
264		}
265		if len(params.TagIDs) > 0 {
266			whereClauses = append(whereClauses, fmt.Sprintf(
267				"EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM service_tags st WHERE st.service_id = s.id AND st.tag_id = ANY($%d::uuid[]))", argIdx))
268			args = append(args, pq.Array(params.TagIDs))
269			argIdx++
270		}
271		if params.MinPrice != nil {
272			whereClauses = append(whereClauses, fmt.Sprintf("s.price_cents >= $%d", argIdx))
273			args = append(args, *params.MinPrice)
274			argIdx++
275		}
276		if params.MaxPrice != nil {
277			whereClauses = append(whereClauses, fmt.Sprintf("s.price_cents <= $%d", argIdx))
278			args = append(args, *params.MaxPrice)
279			argIdx++
280		}
281		if params.MinRating != nil {
282			whereClauses = append(whereClauses, fmt.Sprintf("s.rating_avg >= $%d", argIdx))
283			args = append(args, *params.MinRating)
284			argIdx++
285		}
286		if params.RadiusMiles != nil {
287			whereClauses = append(whereClauses, fmt.Sprintf(
288				"pp.service_area_miles IS NOT NULL AND pp.service_area_miles >= $%d", argIdx))
289			args = append(args, *params.RadiusMiles)
290			argIdx++
291		}
292		if params.AvailDay != nil {
293			availClause := fmt.Sprintf("saw.day_of_week = $%d", argIdx)
294			args = append(args, *params.AvailDay)
295			argIdx++
296	
297			if params.AvailTo != "" {
298				availClause += fmt.Sprintf(" AND saw.end_time >= $%d::time", argIdx)
299				args = append(args, params.AvailTo)
300				argIdx++
301			}
302	
303			whereClauses = append(whereClauses, fmt.Sprintf(
304				"EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM service_availability_windows saw WHERE saw.service_id = s.id AND %s)", availClause))
305		}
306	
307		if len(params.ExcludeProviderUserIDs) > 0 {
308			whereClauses = append(whereClauses, fmt.Sprintf("pp.user_id != ALL($%d::uuid[])", argIdx))
309			args = append(args, pq.Array(params.ExcludeProviderUserIDs))
310			argIdx++
311		}
312	
313		whereSQL := strings.Join(whereClauses, " AND ")
314	
315		// ORDER BY
316		var orderSQL string
317		switch params.Sort {
318		case "relevance":
319			if qArgIdx > 0 {
320				orderSQL = fmt.Sprintf(
321					"ORDER BY GREATEST(similarity(s.title, $%d), similarity(pp.business_name, $%d)) DESC, s.created_at DESC",
322					qArgIdx, qArgIdx)
323			} else {
324				orderSQL = "ORDER BY s.created_at DESC"
325			}
326		case "price_asc":
327			orderSQL = "ORDER BY s.price_cents ASC, s.created_at DESC"
328		case "price_desc":
329			orderSQL = "ORDER BY s.price_cents DESC, s.created_at DESC"
330		case "popularity":
331			orderSQL = "ORDER BY s.popularity_score DESC, s.created_at DESC"
332		case "rating":
333			orderSQL = "ORDER BY s.rating_avg DESC, s.created_at DESC"
334		case "distance":
335			// Local proxy: smaller service_area_miles = more local. NULLs sort last.
336			orderSQL = "ORDER BY pp.service_area_miles ASC NULLS LAST, s.created_at DESC"
337		default: // newest
338			orderSQL = "ORDER BY s.created_at DESC"
339		}
340	
341		// Count query
342		countSQL := fmt.Sprintf(
343			`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM services s
344			 LEFT JOIN categories c ON s.category_id = c.id
345			 JOIN provider_profiles pp ON s.provider_id = pp.id
346			 WHERE %s`, whereSQL)
347	
348		var total int
349		if err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, countSQL, args...).Scan(&total); err != nil {
350			return nil, fmt.Errorf("search: count: %w", err)
351		}
352	
353		// Data query
354		offset := (params.Page - 1) * params.PageSize
355	
356		limitArg := argIdx
357		args = append(args, params.PageSize)
358		argIdx++
359		offsetArg := argIdx
360		args = append(args, offset)
361	
362		dataSQL := fmt.Sprintf(
363			`SELECT s.id, s.title, s.description, s.price_cents, s.rating_avg::text,
364			        s.popularity_score, s.status, s.created_at::text, s.updated_at::text,
365			        c.id, c.name, pp.id, pp.business_name, pp.service_area_miles
366			 FROM services s
367			 LEFT JOIN categories c ON s.category_id = c.id
368			 JOIN provider_profiles pp ON s.provider_id = pp.id
369			 WHERE %s
370			 %s
371			 LIMIT $%d OFFSET $%d`, whereSQL, orderSQL, limitArg, offsetArg)
372	
373		rows, err := s.db.QueryContext(ctx, dataSQL, args...)
374		if err != nil {
375			return nil, fmt.Errorf("search: query: %w", err)
376		}
377		defer rows.Close()
378	
379		services := make([]ServiceRow, 0)
380		var serviceIDs []string
381	
382		for rows.Next() {
383			var row ServiceRow
384			var catID, catName *string
385			var provID, provBizName string
386			var provAreaMiles *int
387	
388			if err := rows.Scan(
389				&row.ID, &row.Title, &row.Description, &row.PriceCents,
390				&row.RatingAvg, &row.PopularityScore, &row.Status,
391				&row.CreatedAt, &row.UpdatedAt,
392				&catID, &catName,
393				&provID, &provBizName, &provAreaMiles,
394			); err != nil {
395				return nil, fmt.Errorf("search: scan: %w", err)
396			}
397	
398			if catID != nil {
399				row.Category = &CatRef{ID: *catID, Name: *catName}
400			}
401			row.Provider = ProvRef{ID: provID, BusinessName: provBizName, ServiceAreaMiles: provAreaMiles}
402			row.Tags = make([]TagRef, 0)
403	
404			services = append(services, row)
405			serviceIDs = append(serviceIDs, row.ID)
406		}
407		if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
408			return nil, fmt.Errorf("search: iterate: %w", err)
409		}
410	
411		// Load tags
412		tagMap, err := s.loadServiceTags(ctx, serviceIDs)
413		if err != nil {
414			return nil, err
415		}
416		for i := range services {
417			if tags, ok := tagMap[services[i].ID]; ok {
418				services[i].Tags = tags
419			}
420		}
421	
422		result := &SearchResult{
423			Services: services,
424			Total:    total,
425			Page:     params.Page,
426			PageSize: params.PageSize,
427		}
428	
429		s.cache.Set(key, result)
430		return result, nil
431	}
432	
433	// loadServiceTags batch-loads tags for the given service IDs.
434	func (s *Service) loadServiceTags(ctx context.Context, serviceIDs []string) (map[string][]TagRef, error) {
435		result := make(map[string][]TagRef)
436		if len(serviceIDs) == 0 {
437			return result, nil
438		}
439	
440		rows, err := s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
441			`SELECT st.service_id, t.id, t.name
442			 FROM tags t
443			 JOIN service_tags st ON st.tag_id = t.id
444			 WHERE st.service_id = ANY($1::uuid[])`,
445			pq.Array(serviceIDs))
446		if err != nil {
447			return nil, fmt.Errorf("search: load tags: %w", err)
448		}
449		defer rows.Close()
450	
451		for rows.Next() {
452			var serviceID, tagID, tagName string
453			if err := rows.Scan(&serviceID, &tagID, &tagName); err != nil {
454				return nil, fmt.Errorf("search: scan tag: %w", err)
455			}
456			result[serviceID] = append(result[serviceID], TagRef{ID: tagID, Name: tagName})
457		}
458		return result, rows.Err()
459	}
460	
461	// ---------- search recording ----------
462	
463	// RecordSearch inserts into search_events and (if queryText is non-empty)
464	// search_history. Safe to call from a goroutine.
465	func (s *Service) RecordSearch(ctx context.Context, userID, queryText string, filters map[string]interface{}, resultCount int) {
466		filtersJSON, _ := json.Marshal(filters)
467	
468		_, _ = s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
469			`INSERT INTO search_events (user_id, query_text, filters, result_count) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4)`,
470			userID, queryText, filtersJSON, resultCount)
471	
472		if strings.TrimSpace(queryText) != "" {
473			_, _ = s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
474				`INSERT INTO search_history (user_id, query_text) VALUES ($1, $2)`,
475				userID, queryText)
476		}
477	}
478	
479	// ---------- search history ----------
480	
481	// GetSearchHistory returns the most recent unique search queries for a user.
482	func (s *Service) GetSearchHistory(ctx context.Context, userID string, limit int) ([]SearchHistoryEntry, error) {
483		rows, err := s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
484			`SELECT id, query_text, created_at::text FROM (
485				SELECT DISTINCT ON (query_text) id, query_text, created_at
486				FROM search_history WHERE user_id = $1
487				ORDER BY query_text, created_at DESC
488			) sub ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT $2`,
489			userID, limit)
490		if err != nil {
491			return nil, fmt.Errorf("search: history: %w", err)
492		}
493		defer rows.Close()
494	
495		entries := make([]SearchHistoryEntry, 0)
496		for rows.Next() {
497			var e SearchHistoryEntry
498			if err := rows.Scan(&e.ID, &e.QueryText, &e.CreatedAt); err != nil {
499				return nil, fmt.Errorf("search: scan history: %w", err)
500			}
501			entries = append(entries, e)
502		}
503		return entries, rows.Err()
504	}

506	// ---------- trending ----------
507	
508	// GetTrending returns the top trending services based on recent favorites and
509	// popularity score.
510	func (s *Service) GetTrending(ctx context.Context, limit int) ([]ServiceRow, error) {
511		rows, err := s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
512			`SELECT s.id, s.title, s.description, s.price_cents, s.rating_avg::text,
513			        s.popularity_score, s.status, s.created_at::text, s.updated_at::text,
514			        c.id, c.name, pp.id, pp.business_name, pp.service_area_miles,
515			        COALESCE(fav.fav_count, 0) + s.popularity_score AS trend_score
516			 FROM services s
517			 LEFT JOIN categories c ON s.category_id = c.id
518			 JOIN provider_profiles pp ON s.provider_id = pp.id
519			 LEFT JOIN (
520			     SELECT service_id, COUNT(*) AS fav_count
521			     FROM favorites
522			     WHERE created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
523			     GROUP BY service_id
524			 ) fav ON fav.service_id = s.id
525			 WHERE s.status = 'active'
526			 ORDER BY trend_score DESC, s.created_at DESC
527			 LIMIT $1`, limit)
528		if err != nil {
529			return nil, fmt.Errorf("search: trending: %w", err)
530		}
531		defer rows.Close()
532	
533		services := make([]ServiceRow, 0)
534		var serviceIDs []string
535	
536		for rows.Next() {
537			var row ServiceRow
538			var catID, catName *string
539			var provID, provBizName string
540			var provAreaMiles *int
541			var trendScore int
542	
543			if err := rows.Scan(
544				&row.ID, &row.Title, &row.Description, &row.PriceCents,
545				&row.RatingAvg, &row.PopularityScore, &row.Status,
546				&row.CreatedAt, &row.UpdatedAt,
547				&catID, &catName,
548				&provID, &provBizName, &provAreaMiles,
549				&trendScore,
550			); err != nil {
551				return nil, fmt.Errorf("search: scan trending: %w", err)
552			}
553	
554			if catID != nil {
555				row.Category = &CatRef{ID: *catID, Name: *catName}
556			}
557			row.Provider = ProvRef{ID: provID, BusinessName: provBizName, ServiceAreaMiles: provAreaMiles}
558			row.Tags = make([]TagRef, 0)
559	
560			services = append(services, row)
561			serviceIDs = append(serviceIDs, row.ID)
562		}
563		if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
564			return nil, fmt.Errorf("search: iterate trending: %w", err)
565		}
566	
567		tagMap, err := s.loadServiceTags(ctx, serviceIDs)
568		if err != nil {
569			return nil, err
570		}
571		for i := range services {
572			if tags, ok := tagMap[services[i].ID]; ok {
573				services[i].Tags = tags
574			}
575		}
576	
577		return services, nil
578	}

1	package auth
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"crypto/rand"
6		"crypto/sha256"
7		"database/sql"
8		"encoding/hex"
9		"fmt"
10		"net/http"
11		"os"
12		"strings"
13		"time"
14	
15		"fieldserve/internal/audit"
16		"fieldserve/internal/platform/httpx"
17	
18		"github.com/labstack/echo/v4"
19		"golang.org/x/crypto/bcrypt"
20	)
21	
22	const (
23		cookieName       = "fieldserve_session"
24		idleTimeout      = 8 * time.Hour
25		absoluteLifetime = 7 * 24 * time.Hour
26	)
27	
28	// User represents an authenticated user with their roles.
29	type User struct {
30		ID       string   `json:"id"`
31		Username string   `json:"username"`
32		Email    string   `json:"email"`
33		Roles    []string `json:"roles"`
34	}
35	
36	// Service provides authentication and session management.
37	type Service struct {
38		db    *sql.DB
39		audit *audit.Service
40	}
41	
42	// NewService creates a new auth service.
43	func NewService(db *sql.DB, audit *audit.Service) *Service {
44		return &Service{db: db, audit: audit}
45	}
46	
47	// Authenticate verifies username and password. Returns nil (not error) if
48	// credentials are invalid.
49	func (s *Service) Authenticate(ctx context.Context, username, password string) (*User, error) {
50		var userID, email, passwordHash string
51		err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
52			`SELECT id, email, password_hash FROM users WHERE username = $1`,
53			username,
54		).Scan(&userID, &email, &passwordHash)
55		if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
56			return nil, nil
57		}
58		if err != nil {
59			return nil, fmt.Errorf("auth: query user: %w", err)
60		}
61	
62		if err := bcrypt.CompareHashAndPassword([]byte(passwordHash), []byte(password)); err != nil {
63			return nil, nil
64		}
65	
66		roles, err := s.getUserRoles(ctx, userID)
67		if err != nil {
68			return nil, err
69		}
70	
71		return &User{
72			ID:       userID,
73			Username: username,
74			Email:    email,
75			Roles:    roles,
76		}, nil
77	}
78	
79	// CreateSession generates a new session token, stores its SHA-256 hash in the
80	// database, and returns the raw token.
81	func (s *Service) CreateSession(ctx context.Context, userID string) (string, error) {
82		tokenBytes := make([]byte, 32)
83		if _, err := rand.Read(tokenBytes); err != nil {
84			return "", fmt.Errorf("auth: generate token: %w", err)
85		}
86		token := hex.EncodeToString(tokenBytes)
87	
88		hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(token))
89		tokenHash := hex.EncodeToString(hash[:])
90	
91		_, err := s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
92			`INSERT INTO auth_sessions (user_id, token_hash, expires_at, last_active_at) VALUES ($1, $2, NOW() + INTERVAL '7 days', NOW())`,
93			userID, tokenHash,
94		)
95		if err != nil {
96			return "", fmt.Errorf("auth: create session: %w", err)
97		}
98	
99		return token, nil
100	}
101	
102	// ResolveSession looks up a session by SHA-256(token), checks idle timeout
103	// (8h) and absolute lifetime (7d), updates last_active_at, and returns the
104	// user. Returns nil if expired or invalid.
105	func (s *Service) ResolveSession(ctx context.Context, token string) (*User, error) {
106		hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(token))
107		tokenHash := hex.EncodeToString(hash[:])
108	
109		var userID string
110		var createdAt, lastActiveAt time.Time
111		err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
112			`SELECT user_id, created_at, last_active_at FROM auth_sessions WHERE token_hash = $1`,
113			tokenHash,
114		).Scan(&userID, &createdAt, &lastActiveAt)
115		if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
116			return nil, nil
117		}
118		if err != nil {
119			return nil, fmt.Errorf("auth: resolve session: %w", err)
120		}
121	
122		now := time.Now()
123		if now.Sub(lastActiveAt) > idleTimeout || now.Sub(createdAt) > absoluteLifetime {
124			// Session expired — clean it up
125			_, _ = s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
126				`DELETE FROM auth_sessions WHERE token_hash = $1`, tokenHash,
127			)
128			return nil, nil
129		}
130	
131		// Update last_active_at
132		_, _ = s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
133			`UPDATE auth_sessions SET last_active_at = NOW() WHERE token_hash = $1`, tokenHash,
134		)
135	
136		var username, email string
137		err = s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
138			`SELECT username, email FROM users WHERE id = $1`, userID,
139		).Scan(&username, &email)
140		if err != nil {
141			return nil, fmt.Errorf("auth: resolve user: %w", err)
142		}
143	
144		roles, err := s.getUserRoles(ctx, userID)
145		if err != nil {
146			return nil, err
147		}
148	
149		return &User{
150			ID:       userID,
151			Username: username,
152			Email:    email,
153			Roles:    roles,
154		}, nil
155	}
156	
157	// InvalidateSession deletes a session by its token hash.
158	func (s *Service) InvalidateSession(ctx context.Context, token string) error {
159		hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(token))
160		tokenHash := hex.EncodeToString(hash[:])
161	
162		_, err := s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
163			`DELETE FROM auth_sessions WHERE token_hash = $1`, tokenHash,
164		)
165		if err != nil {
166			return fmt.Errorf("auth: invalidate session: %w", err)
167		}
168		return nil
169	}
170	
171	// InvalidateUserSessions deletes all sessions for a user.
172	func (s *Service) InvalidateUserSessions(ctx context.Context, userID string) error {
173		_, err := s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
174			`DELETE FROM auth_sessions WHERE user_id = $1`, userID,
175		)
176		if err != nil {
177			return fmt.Errorf("auth: invalidate user sessions: %w", err)
178		}
179		return nil
180	}
181	
182	// BootstrapAdmin creates the first admin user, role, and profile in a
183	// transaction. Returns a conflict error if an admin already exists.
184	func (s *Service) BootstrapAdmin(ctx context.Context, username, password, email string) (*User, error) {
185		tx, err := s.db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
186		if err != nil {
187			return nil, fmt.Errorf("auth: begin tx: %w", err)
188		}
189		defer tx.Rollback()
190	
191		// Check if an administrator role assignment already exists
192		var count int
193		err = tx.QueryRowContext(ctx,
194			`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM user_roles ur JOIN roles r ON ur.role_id = r.id WHERE r.name = 'administrator'`,
195		).Scan(&count)
196		if err != nil {
197			return nil, fmt.Errorf("auth: check admin: %w", err)
198		}
199		if count > 0 {
200			return nil, httpx.NewConflictError("admin_exists", "An administrator already exists.")
201		}
202	
203		// Hash password
204		hashed, err := bcrypt.GenerateFromPassword([]byte(password), bcrypt.DefaultCost)
205		if err != nil {
206			return nil, fmt.Errorf("auth: hash password: %w", err)
207		}
208	
209		// Create user
210		var userID string
211		err = tx.QueryRowContext(ctx,
212			`INSERT INTO users (username, password_hash, email) VALUES ($1, $2, $3) RETURNING id`,
213			username, string(hashed), email,
214		).Scan(&userID)
215		if err != nil {
216			return nil, fmt.Errorf("auth: create user: %w", err)
217		}
218	
219		// Get or create administrator role
220		var roleID string
221		err = tx.QueryRowContext(ctx,
222			`SELECT id FROM roles WHERE name = 'administrator'`,
223		).Scan(&roleID)
224		if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
225			err = tx.QueryRowContext(ctx,
226				`INSERT INTO roles (name, description) VALUES ('administrator', 'Full system access') RETURNING id`,
227			).Scan(&roleID)
228		}
229		if err != nil {
230			return nil, fmt.Errorf("auth: get/create role: %w", err)
231		}
232	
233		// Assign role
234		_, err = tx.ExecContext(ctx,
235			`INSERT INTO user_roles (user_id, role_id) VALUES ($1, $2)`,
236			userID, roleID,
237		)
238		if err != nil {
239			return nil, fmt.Errorf("auth: assign role: %w", err)
240		}
241	
242		// Create profile
243		_, err = tx.ExecContext(ctx,
244			`INSERT INTO admin_profiles (user_id, display_name) VALUES ($1, $2)`,
245			userID, username,
246		)
247		if err != nil {
248			return nil, fmt.Errorf("auth: create profile: %w", err)
249		}
250	
251		if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
252			return nil, fmt.Errorf("auth: commit: %w", err)
253		}
254	
255		return &User{
256			ID:       userID,
257			Username: username,
258			Email:    email,
259			Roles:    []string{"administrator"},
260		}, nil
261	}
262	
263	// getUserRoles fetches role names for a user.
264	func (s *Service) getUserRoles(ctx context.Context, userID string) ([]string, error) {
265		rows, err := s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
266			`SELECT r.name FROM roles r JOIN user_roles ur ON r.id = ur.role_id WHERE ur.user_id = $1`,
267			userID,
268		)
269		if err != nil {
270			return nil, fmt.Errorf("auth: query roles: %w", err)
271		}
272		defer rows.Close()
273	
274		var roles []string
275		for rows.Next() {
276			var role string
277			if err := rows.Scan(&role); err != nil {
278				return nil, fmt.Errorf("auth: scan role: %w", err)
279			}
280			roles = append(roles, role)
281		}
282		if roles == nil {
283			roles = []string{}
284		}
285		return roles, rows.Err()
286	}
287	
288	// cookieSecure determines whether the Secure flag should be set on cookies.
289	// It reads the COOKIE_SECURE environment variable:
290	//   - "true"  → always Secure (for production / TLS termination)
291	//   - "false" → never Secure (for local HTTP development)
292	//   - "auto" or unset → Secure when the request arrived over TLS
293	func cookieSecure(c echo.Context) bool {
294		val := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv("COOKIE_SECURE")))
295		switch val {
296		case "true":
297			return true
298		case "false":
299			return false
300		default: // "auto" or unset
301			return c.Request().TLS != nil ||
302				c.Request().Header.Get("X-Forwarded-Proto") == "https"
303		}
304	}
305	
306	// SetSessionCookie sets the session cookie on the response.
307	func (s *Service) SetSessionCookie(c echo.Context, token string) {
308		c.SetCookie(&http.Cookie{
309			Name:     cookieName,
310			Value:    token,
311			Path:     "/",
312			HttpOnly: true,
313			SameSite: http.SameSiteStrictMode,
314			Secure:   cookieSecure(c),
315		})
316	}
317	
318	// ClearSessionCookie clears the session cookie.
319	func (s *Service) ClearSessionCookie(c echo.Context) {
320		c.SetCookie(&http.Cookie{
321			Name:     cookieName,
322			Value:    "",
323			Path:     "/",
324			HttpOnly: true,
325			SameSite: http.SameSiteStrictMode,
326			Secure:   cookieSecure(c),
327			MaxAge:   -1,
328		})
329	}
330	
331	// GetSessionToken reads the session token from the cookie.
332	func (s *Service) GetSessionToken(c echo.Context) string {
333		cookie, err := c.Cookie(cookieName)
334		if err != nil {
335			return ""
336		}
337		return cookie.Value
338	}
339	
340	// UserFromContext gets the authenticated user from the echo context.
341	func UserFromContext(c echo.Context) *User {
342		u, ok := c.Get("auth_user").(*User)
343		if !ok {
344			return nil
345		}
346		return u
347	}
